name: libreoffice-connector
description: Drive LibreOffice from Claude: read/write Calc sheets, run macros, take window screenshots — 61 tools. Auto-launches LibreOffice.
long_description: Connects Claude to a running (or auto-launched) LibreOffice via the UNO API. 61 tools: document lifecycle, Calc data/formulas/formatting/charts/validation, Writer text and tables, drawing shapes, embedded Basic macros (run/inspect/replace), saved-file XML inspection, real window screenshots, and a raw UNO escape hatch. A Node.js launcher (index.js) hands stdio to the server process, which runs under LibreOffice's own bundled Python — the only interpreter that ships the required 'uno' module. Requires a local LibreOffice installation; no other dependencies, no telemetry.

libreoffice_python.title: LibreOffice bundled Python (auto-detected — usually leave empty)
libreoffice_python.description: Only set this if LibreOffice is installed somewhere unusual. When empty, the launcher finds it automatically (Windows: C:\Program Files\LibreOffice\program\python.exe — macOS: /Applications/LibreOffice.app/Contents/Resources/python — Linux: /usr/bin/python3 with python3-uno).

uno_port.title: UNO socket port (advanced)
uno_port.description: Localhost port the server uses to reach LibreOffice (it auto-launches LibreOffice with this port when none is listening). Leave the default unless 2002 is taken.
